chapters.title: Greetings & Introductions
chapters.description: Learn basic German greetings and how to introduce yourself.

lessons.explanation: 'Guten Morgen' = Good morning. 'Guten Tag' = Good day/afternoon. 'Guten Abend' = Good evening.
'Ich heiße' = My name is / I am called. From the verb 'heißen'.

lessons.hint: 'Wie' = how, 'geht es' = goes it, 'dir' = to you (informal dative).

lessons.prompt: Which phrase means 'Nice to meet you'?
lessons.explanation: 'Freut mich' = Pleased to meet you. Literally 'it pleases me'.

lessons.prompt: Ich komme ___ Indien. (I come from India.)
lessons.explanation: 'Aus' = from (for cities and countries). 'Ich komme aus...' = I come from...

chapters.title: German Articles & Genders
chapters.description: Master der, die, das and understand German noun genders.

lessons.prompt: What is the correct article for 'Mann' (man)?
lessons.explanation: 'Der Mann' = the man. Masculine nouns use 'der'. There's no perfect rule — memorize with each noun.
'Kind' (child) is neuter → 'das'. 'Das Kind' = the child.

lessons.prompt: Which is the correct indefinite article for a feminine noun?
lessons.explanation: Indefinite articles: ein (masc/neut) → eine (fem). 'Eine Frau' = a woman.

lessons.prompt: Ich habe ___ Hund. (I have a dog.)
lessons.explanation: 'Hund' is masculine. In accusative case (direct object), 'ein' → 'einen' for masculine nouns.

chapters.title: Numbers & Shopping
chapters.description: Count and use numbers while shopping in German.

lessons.explanation: 1-12: eins, zwei, drei, vier, fünf, sechs, sieben, acht, neun, zehn, elf, zwölf.

lessons.prompt: Das kostet ___ Euro. (That costs five euros.)
lessons.explanation: 'Fünf' = five. In German, 'kostet' (costs) is used for prices.
Tens in German: zwanzig (20), dreißig (30), vierzig (40), fünfzig (50), sechzig (60).

lessons.prompt: Ich möchte ___ Äpfel kaufen. (I would like to buy three apples.)
lessons.explanation: 'Drei' = three. 'Ich möchte' = I would like. 'Kaufen' = to buy.

chapters.title: Food & Ordering
chapters.description: Order meals and drinks at a German restaurant or café.

lessons.explanation: 'Ich hätte gerne' = I would like. Very polite way to order food. Hätte = would have (Konjunktiv II).

lessons.prompt: Ich trinke gerne ___. (I like to drink coffee.)
lessons.explanation: 'Kaffee' = coffee. 'Gerne' expresses liking. 'Ich trinke gerne' = I like to drink.

lessons.hint: 'Rechnung' = bill/check. 'Bitte' = please. Very important restaurant phrase.

lessons.explanation: 'Frühstück' = breakfast (literally 'early piece'). 'Mittagessen' = lunch, 'Abendessen' = dinner.

lessons.prompt: Haben Sie einen Tisch für ___ Personen? (Do you have a table for two?)
lessons.explanation: 'Zwei Personen' = two people/persons. 'Haben Sie' = do you have (formal).

chapters.title: Directions & Transport
chapters.description: Ask for and give directions, and navigate public transport.

lessons.explanation: 'Geradeaus' = straight ahead. 'Links' = left, 'rechts' = right, 'zurück' = back.

lessons.prompt: Wo ist die nächste ___-Station? (Where is the nearest U-Bahn station?)
lessons.explanation: 'U-Bahn' = underground/metro. 'S-Bahn' = suburban train. 'Bushaltestelle' = bus stop.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Excuse me, where is the train station?'

lessons.explanation: 'Einbahnstraße' = one-way street. Literally 'one-way-road'.

lessons.prompt: Der Zug fährt in ___ Minuten ab. (The train departs in ten minutes.)
lessons.explanation: 'Zehn' = ten. 'Abfahren' = to depart. 'In zehn Minuten' = in ten minutes.
